mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue momentum; higher INDPRO growth predicts positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive INDPRO growth months do not exceed negative growth months with t-test p > 0.05, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or the mean return in triggered months is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in payroll employment signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income and corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month and PAYEMS falls month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE change and PAYEMS change does not add incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) versus individual effects alone, or 60-day returns are positive in > 45% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an acceleration in consumer price index inflation intensifies expectations of further central bank rate hikes, raising the equity risk premium and generating negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is stable or rising, positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes do not correlate with negative 20-day returns (Rank IC > 0 over a 3-year window), or returns are positive in > 55% of high-inflation months during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales captures near-term consumer spending momentum; positive RSAFS changes predict positive S&P 500 forward 5-day returns as markets reprice earnings expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive RSAFS growth do not outperform negative growth days (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market slack that undermines consumer spending and corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of months when UNRATE > 5.0%, or the mean return in triggered months is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a declining industrial production index and an elevated federal funds rate signals a contractionary environment where financing costs rise while output falls, jointly depressing corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition does not produce more negative returns than either variable alone (ANOVA interaction p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in > 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1,500 thousand units indicate a cooling residential construction sector that reduces aggregate demand and construction-related corporate revenues, predicting negative S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following HOUST < 1500 are not significantly negative compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in > 55% of triggered weeks.
